input.prompt:
I have a .NET 10 app running on Linux (PID 12345) and I need to set up

Help me figure out which runtime it's using and configure dumps accordingly.

data.content:
PID 12345 isn't running on this machine, so I can't inspect it directly. Here's how to do it yourself and configure dumps for either runtime:

## 1. Identify the Runtime

```bash
# Check for CoreCLR — if libcoreclr.so is loaded, it's CoreCLR
cat /proc/12345/maps | grep -q libcoreclr && echo "CoreCLR" || echo "Likely NativeAOT"

# Alternative: check if dotnet host is involved
ls -l /proc/12345/exe   # NativeAOT → your app binary; CoreCLR → often dotnet
```

## 2. Configure Crash Dumps

### If CoreCLR:
```bash
# Set environment variables for the process (e.g. in systemd unit or shell):
export DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1
export DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4          # 4 = Full dump (1=Normal, 2=WithHeap, 3=Triage)
export DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/tmp/dumps/coredump.%p.%t
mkdir -p /tmp/dumps
```
CoreCLR has its own built-in dump writer — these `DOTNET_Dbg*` variables control it independently of the OS.

### If NativeAOT:
NativeAOT apps don't have the managed dump infrastructure. Use the **OS-level core dump** mechanism:

```bash
# Enable core dumps
ulimit -c unlimited

# Configure kernel core pattern (requires root)
echo '/tmp/dumps/core.%e.%p.%t' | sudo tee /proc/sys/kernel/core_pattern

# Or use systemd-coredump (if systemd is managing cores)
sudo coredumpctl list   # verify it's active
mkdir -p /tmp/dumps
```

### If Using systemd (either runtime):
Add to your service unit:
```ini
[Service]
LimitCORE=infinity
Environment=DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1      # only matters for CoreCLR
Environment=DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4
```

## Quick Reference

| Feature | CoreCLR | NativeAOT |
|---|---|---|
| `libcoreclr.so` loaded | ✅ | ❌ |
| `DOTNET_Dbg*` env vars | ✅ work | ❌ ignored |
| OS core dumps (`ulimit -c`) | ✅ works | ✅ required |
| `createdump` tool available | ✅ | ❌ |
| `dotnet-dump` analysis | ✅ full | ⚠️ limited |

Run the identification commands on your actual host, then apply the matching configuration.
